title: parallel-map worker named-let loops eat stack per iteration: SIGBUS/SIGILL at ~5-8k iterations
goal: Found by the P4 stress harness (2026-07-02, master 2692ca63). A named-let loop inside a parallel-map worker closure consumes native stack per iteration on the pool-thread execution path (the loop is TCO'd when called directly, via map, or via parallel-map with <= 3 items which runs inline). With 8 items: 5000 iters/worker OK, 8000 SIGBUS (rc 138), 20000+ SIGILL (rc 132); with 150000 iters a 3-item list passes and a 4-item list crashes. Default 512KB pthread stacks x per-iteration frames ~ the observed threshold. Either the worker codegen path must preserve TCO or pool threads need the loop-frame fix from bug-RR (isTcoLoopAlloca) applied to the worker trampoline.
acceptance: tests/stress/found/parallel_worker_loop_20k.esk completes with OK under -r and AOT
campaign_payoff: Silently caps how much work a parallel-map worker may do; blocks the 8-workers-x-heavy-closure P4 scenario at real workloads.
